verdict_reasoning: STIM1 (Stromal Interaction Molecule 1) is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein resident primarily in the ER membrane, where it senses luminal Ca2+ via its EF-hand domain. Upon ER Ca2+ depletion, STIM1 oligomerizes and translocates to ER-PM junctions where it physically couples to and activates ORAI1 channels at the plasma membrane. Critically, STIM1 itself has a documented stable plasma membrane pool (detected by surface biotinylation and flow cytometry on intact cells) with its N-terminal EF-hand domain facing the extracellular space. This PM-resident fraction constitutes a genuine surface-accessible pool with an extracellular domain, qualifying as a classical single-pass TM protein at the plasma membrane.
